calendar.matters:
RESPONDENT ANNA WONG HOMESTEAD EXEMPTION DECLARATION
Off Calendar Feb-08-2023 Off Calendar

Homestead Exemption Declaration
Off Calendar Feb-10-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES
John Kithas, 415.788.8100 of the Law Ofices of John A. Kithas appearing via Zoom for Petitioner John Kithas.
Ester Adut, 650.469.2456 appearing for respondents Anna wong and Larry Wong.
Winnie Chan, Certified Cantonese Court Interpreter.

Counsel for the Respondents requests a Statement of Decision. Ms. Adut list her reasons why she is contesting the tentative ruling. Counsel also requests that her clients are allowed to give testimony. The Court requests an offer of proof. Ms. Adut's states her offer of proof re: homestead exemption. The Court rejects the declaration of homestead exemption as not timely. No testimony will be heard, only arguments regarding the Court's tentative ruling.

Ms. Adut requests that the Court take judicial notice of Jun-14-22 motion for sale of dwelling and the Sep-12-22 response of Anna Wong to motion for sale of dwelling. Counsel also wants the 2023 assessors records admitted.

The matter is submitted. The Court rules as follows: the requests for judical notice are denied as a) irrevlant, b) cumulative and c) untimely. The request for a statement of decision is denied in it's entirety.

Hearing held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:RESPONDENT ANNA WONG Homestead Exemption Declaration is DENIED. Respondent provides no relevant evidence to support any claim of surprise, mistake or excusable neglect for the judgment debtors not to appear at the September 14, 2022 hearing re: sale of dwelling individually, Larry Wong and Anna Wong, or via their counsel, Ester Adut. Judgement debtor had notice of the continued hearing re: sale of dwelling and could appear in-person (previously appeared in-person), via Zoom (previously appeared via Zoom) or properly via CourtCall. No explanation is provided for failure to appear via Zoom (video or phone).

Additionally, the Court's order after September 14, 2022 hearing re: sale of dwelling is based on multiple briefs filed over a period of 4 months. The written submissions discussed the homestead exemption and the ruling on September 14, 2022 necessarily included a ruling on the homestead claim as is evidenced by the final order. The ruling at September 14, 2022 hearing was based on multiple written submissions of the parties for which the Court granted several continuances to make sure that both sides had ample opportunity to present their evidence and arguments in writing.

There is no evidence provided to support a possibly different outcome (given extensive briefing and several continued hearings) had defendant appeared in-person, over Zoom (video or phone) or properly over the CourtCall instead of a CourtCall line designated for non-participating observers (The Court's web-site clearly states: "San Francisco Superior Court has established listen-only telephone lines to allow remote access to public court proceedings."), which is apparently the number respondent's counsel used to appear at the hearing.

As stated above, the Court continued prior hearings re: sale of dwelling, at which the judgment debtor was present, specifically for the purpose of giving everyone every opportunity to present their evidence and arguments in writing and to give the parties more time to collect such evidence.

The Court further notes that it appears that no notice that the tentative ruling for September 14, 2022 was contested. The Court's September 14, 2022 tentative ruling, based on voluminous written submissions, was uncontested.

Therefore, on the current request for relief via Homestead Exemption Declaration, the Court rules that the determination of the Court based on evidence, briefs and oral arguments submitted to the Court before September 14, 2022 should not be modified.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported.

NOTE: During roll call, this matter was called at least three separate times to see if anyone would be appearing. Each time, no appearances were stated. The court reporter and court interpreter were excused. At 10:51am, after the conclusion of the morning calendar, respondent's counsel Ester Adut called Department 501 and left a voicemail stating that she was on CourtCall and was still waiting for her matter to be called. When her call was returned, Ms. Adut stated that she did not see the court's tentative ruling or give notice to opposing counsel that she would be appearing in court to argue the motion. Ms. Adut further stated that she could not provide a confirmation of her CourtCall registration, because she called the public access number listed on the court's website. Ms. Adut was informed at that time that the number she dialed is a listen-only telephone line intended to allow remote access to public court proceedings.
